name: JDS Labs Element II
quirks: USB endpoint is playback-only for the computer; RCA analog input is not a capture source.
384 kHz PCM is capability metadata, not an automatic desktop target. Keep normal app graphs at 48 kHz unless the user intentionally runs a high-rate listening chain.
The low output impedance and high headphone power make it suitable for low-impedance headphones such as TH-X00, but analog gain remains a physical control.
source_notes: JDS Labs documents Element II as an XMOS XU208 USB Audio Class 2 DAC supporting 16/24/32-bit PCM at 44.1, 48, 88.2, 96, 176.4, 192, and 384 kHz on Linux without extra drivers.
JDS Labs documents headphone output plus RCA preamp output, with RCA analog input bypassing the USB DAC path.
